name: Braised Lamb Shank and Escarole Warm Salad
description: Slow-braised halal lamb shanks with a cider vinegar and thyme reduction dressing, served warm over escarole with feta and tomato.
keywords: lamb shank recipe, halal french cooking, warm lamb salad, escarole, braised lamb
recipeIngredient: 2 tsp fresh thyme leaves (or 1/2 tsp dried)
450 g (1 lb) escarole, trimmed and cut into 2.5 cm strips
1 large ripe tomato, cored and cut into thin wedges

recipeInstructions.text: Pat the lamb shanks dry with kitchen paper. Season all surfaces with salt and black pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large heavy casserole over medium-high heat. Sear shanks in two batches, 4-5 minutes per side, until deep mahogany. Remove and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Cook the sliced onion in the same pan for 10-12 minutes until soft and beginning to colour.
Add garlic and cook 90 seconds. Add thyme and dry mustard, stir for 30 seconds.
Deglaze with cider vinegar, scraping up the fond. Let bubble for 1 minute.
Return shanks to the casserole, add 240 ml water. Bring to a gentle simmer, cover, and braise on low heat for 2 to 2.5 hours.
Remove shanks and rest covered. Skim fat from the braising liquid.
Boil the braising liquid over medium-high heat for 5-8 minutes until it coats the back of a spoon. Taste and correct salt.
Place escarole and tomato wedges in a large bowl or platter. Remove lamb from bones in large pieces and arrange over the greens. Pour the hot dressing over immediately.
Crumble feta over the top and serve within ten minutes.
